purpose: The discovery lane's scoreboard: exact-evaluator construction problems with the best KNOWN values at concrete instance sizes. A witsoc construction strictly beating a 'best_known' entry (re-verified by the exact evaluator) is a genuinely novel, machine-checkable result. Values below are conservative reference points from published tables; 'beat' claims require independent re-verification AND a literature check before any external claim (solve-claim discipline applies).

problems.name: sparse rulers (restricted difference bases)
problems.metric: minimum marks |A|, A ⊆ [0..n], all differences 1..n covered

records.source: sparse ruler tables; optimality known up to ~213

problems.honest_note: optimal values are known/verified for n into the hundreds — beating these requires n beyond verified ranges; the near-term goal is MATCHING optimal values automatically, then pushing n past published verification frontiers

problems.name: Erdős minimum overlap problem (finite instances)
problems.metric: min over A (|A|=n in [1..2n]) of M(A) = max_k #{(a,b) in A x comp : a-b=k}

records.source: finite-instance values not tabulated in standard references; asymptotic constant bracketed [0.379005, 0.380926] (Haugland; AlphaEvolve 2025 upper bound 0.380924)

problems.honest_note: the asymptotic constant is the famous quantity; finite-n tables are sparse in the literature — establishing a clean finite-n table with verified optima (exhaustive for small n) is itself a contribution, and the evaluator already verifies any claimed A exactly

problems.name: Sidon sets in [1..n] (B2 sets)
problems.metric: maximum size of a Sidon subset of [1..n]
problems.honest_note: small-n optima long settled; the lane's value here is calibration (engine must MATCH known optima before record attempts elsewhere)

protocol: 1) evolve with the exact evaluator (admissibility violations are errors, never penalties); 2) any candidate record re-verified independently by re-running the evaluator on the witness; 3) literature/novelty check before any claim; 4) ledger entry only with witness + repro; 5) calibration first: the engine must reproduce known optima before any 'beat' attempt counts.
